title: Context-grounded participant handling
highlights: Meeting participants now come from trusted meeting context instead of diarized speaker labels, preventing false participant claims in notes and summaries.
Summary grounding, fact extraction, and visible note rendering now receive context projections tailored to their role, keeping richer Codex context available without overexposing it in notes.
Legacy participant backfill was tightened and new regressions cover transcript-only speaker labels, trusted-context participant extraction, and context-review prompt behavior.

title: Production durability polish
highlights: Realtime transcription failures now surface fallback mode immediately while reconnect attempts continue in the background.
Meeting decisions, action items, open questions, and rename/export snapshots stay synchronized more reliably across durable storage and downstream evidence feeds.
Knowledge enrichment now preserves the intended trust hierarchy by allowing clear private/internal evidence to outrank weaker public-reference disagreement, with new regressions covering the rule.

title: Settings clarity and release history
highlights: Settings now shows useful release TLDR notes all the time, with the latest release visible first and earlier releases available in a bounded scrolling history.
Enrichment-source examples and active connection rows are much denser, so source setup and health are easier to scan without losing important status detail.
Recent concept-memory display filters more low-signal transcript residue, reducing junk concepts that should not be treated as durable knowledge.

title: Recurring concept cleanup
highlights: Automatic recurring-concept enrichment no longer promotes ambiguous single-token transcript leftovers into durable memory on its own.
Suppressed Context Library rows stay hidden from active listings while still remaining available to audit-oriented flows.
Regression coverage was added for observed junk seeds such as API, Hey, MIT, Nice, Very, and XYZ.

title: Grounded meeting-fact precision
highlights: Meeting fact extraction keeps structured accepted context line-oriented instead of flattening separate evidence lines into one blob.
Durable knowledge explanations no longer leak into customer or organization facts as if they were meeting participants.
Markdown regeneration keeps accepted context synchronized without recreating flattened duplicate context blocks.

title: Priority enrichment source planning
highlights: Barn Owl gained clearer product and verification guidance for priority enrichment sources, including how higher-value context should be assembled and evaluated.
CLI and Codex-facing documentation were tightened so enrichment setup, QA, and handoff flows are easier to execute consistently.
Focused tests and source-handoff verification were expanded around the release workflow.

title: Transcript durability and context grounding
highlights: Realtime hinting, transcript context handling, and meeting-fact extraction were strengthened so trusted context stays better aligned with final notes and local artifacts.
Persistence and local library update paths were tightened to keep regenerated content synchronized across stored meeting outputs.
The app, CLI, and test coverage expanded around repair, enrichment, and production-readiness workflows.

title: Meeting evidence export and control surface
highlights: Barn Owl added a richer control/export surface for meeting evidence, allowing downstream consumers and operator workflows to inspect more structured recording state.
Settings, recorder, and menu-bar experiences were broadened alongside control-command and persistence support.
The release included substantial architecture docs and automated coverage for evidence export, enrichment operations, and local durability.

title: Packaging hardening
highlights: Packaging scripts were tightened for Barn Owl app distribution and source handoff generation.
Release artifact creation was made more resilient so later update-feed work had a cleaner base.
